notes: Application is a Streamlit-based AI paper auditing tool (NeurIPS 2026 reproducibility checklist). No relational DB; data model consists of Python dataclasses/Pydantic models (AuditState, ExtractedInfo, ChecklistItem).
Functional category (212K bytes) exceeds 200K splitting threshold → split into functional_writer_backend and functional_writer_frontend, with a consolidator.
All writers use engine=cli per mandatory rule.

writers.scope: Complete data model: all Python dataclasses and Pydantic models (AuditState, ExtractedInfo, ChecklistItem and sub-fields), all configuration structures (AUDIT_CONFIG, CHAT_CONFIG, SOTA_CONFIG dicts with exact field values), all named constants (API keys, model name constants, temperature constants, Semantic Scholar API constants), all enum-like string sets (CHECKLIST_KEYS, checklist answers), all session_state keys with types and lifecycle. This application has no SQL database — the data model is the full set of structured Python types that flow through the audit pipeline. Every field name, type annotation, default value, and constraint must be specified.
writer_guidance: SKIP ## FIX LOG, ## PURGE LOG, ## REFORMAT LOG sections at top of extraction files — these are audit metadata, not spec content.
Preserve [GAP: ...] markers verbatim. Do NOT invent field values.
For each model/dataclass: name, all fields with types, defaults, constraints, and the extraction source file:line.
For LLM response JSON schemas (extraction_prompt return schema, evaluation_prompt return schema, etc.): reproduce the full field list as documented in extracted_backend_core_01.md § 2.2.

The audit result dict ('resultado') has 16 top-level keys matching CHECKLIST_KEYS plus optional keys ('metricas', 'informacion_extraida', 'general_analysis_map', 'original_extraction_raw', 'hybrid_triage_fragments', 'extracted_hyperparameters_hybrid', 'evaluation_signals') — document this as a virtual schema.

AuditState, ExtractedInfo, ChecklistItem: these appear in test files (test_audit_state.py) — recover their full contracts from the cross_ref_root_to_backend.md resolution or mark as [GAP] if unresolved.

writers.scope: Backend functional specification: (1) The 6-phase audit pipeline (PaperAuditor.audit): FASE 1 InformationExtraction, FASE 1.5 HybridHyperparameterExtraction, FASE 2 ReproducibilityEvaluation, FASE 2.5 ChecklistVerification, FASE 3 MetricsCalculation, FASE 4 MetadataAggregation — each phase with exact trigger condition, input context keys, output context keys, error handling, and side effects. (2) All 15 exported skills plus non-exported skills (ChecklistVerificationSkill, HybridHyperparameterExtractionSkill, all regex detection skills not in __init__) — each skill with execute() signature, input context keys required, output keys, guard conditions, and exact error return shapes. (3) PaperChatbot: preguntar() method with conversation history handling. (4) SotaAnalyzer: 5-step SOTA pipeline with SemanticScholar API integration. (5) PDF parser: convert_pdf_to_markdown chunked Docling flow. (6) All business rules: retry logic (LLMClient: 6 total attempts with exact sleep durations), JSON repair patterns, MAP/REDUCE consolidation, fragment sizing formula, balanced JSON extraction algorithm.
writer_guidance: SKIP ## FIX LOG, ## PURGE LOG, ## REFORMAT LOG at top of files.
Preserve all RULE/TRIGGER/CONDITION/ACTION/ERROR structured markers — do NOT flatten to prose.
Preserve [GAP: ...] markers verbatim (e.g. _preprocess_paper is UNRESOLVED — keep as GAP).
For each phase: specify the exact context keys read/written, the skill(s) invoked, error handling paths, and what is returned on success vs failure.
For LLMClient.generate(): document all 6 retry attempts, sleep intervals between retries, and which exception types trigger retry vs immediate fail.

For fragment sizing in InformationExtractionSkill: document the exact formula (total_chars/4, max 3 cuts, fallback to RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter chunk_size=25000, chunk_overlap=2000, first 4 items).
For the balanced JSON extraction loop: document the algorithm (find first '{', stack-count '{'/'}', extract when stack==0).
For SemanticScholarSearchSkill: document the execute({'search_queries': []}) -> {'sota_papers': ...} contract fully.

writers.scope: Frontend functional specification: (1) Application bootstrap flow (app.py): exact order of operations — set_page_config → apply_custom_styles → initialize_session_state → sidebar render → file upload → conditional audit results render → chatbot render. (2) File upload flow (process_uploaded_file): deduplication by MD5 hash, file type branching (PDF via Docling / TXT-MD via open()), auditor.audit() invocation with status_callback, session_state writes, temp file lifecycle. (3) Audit results rendering (render_audit_results): full page layout — success banner, health verdict, 4-column metrics, RAG ficha técnica, compliance table (_build_table_html), three expander sections (Pipeline Análisis Profundo, Extracción Híbrida, Evaluación), download button. (4) Report generation (generate_report): exact Markdown output structure with all 16 checklist row formats including risk annotations. (5) Scoring logic (get_checklist_health): full item evaluation algorithm with pending_justification and missing_evidence flags, alert_msg logic, crowdsourcing special-case suffix. (6) SOTA analysis UI flow (render_sota_analysis). (7) Chatbot UI flow (render_chatbot): message history, submit action, st.rerun(). (8) Session state initialization (initialize_session_state): all 5 keys with guards.

For get_checklist_health: document EVERY rule exactly — pending_justification condition ('no' in answer AND (not is_no_justified OR not justification)), missing_evidence condition ('yes' in answer AND not evidence AND not justification), crowdsourcing special-case alert suffix.
For generate_report: document the exact Markdown template structure including all risk annotation formats.
For process_uploaded_file: document the MD5 deduplication logic, temp/ directory lifecycle, and status_callback pattern.
For render_audit_results: document the 16 CHECKLIST_KEYS and their CHECKLIST_LABELS as presented in the UI.
Cross-reference g_004 through g_008, g_013, g_026, g_027 resolutions from cross_ref_root_to_frontend.md — these fill in the function signatures originally missing from the root cluster.

writers.scope: Technical specification: (1) External API contracts — Google Gemini API (all 6 model constants, generation_config dicts with exact parameter values, response_mime_type, temperature, top_k, top_p, max_output_tokens), Semantic Scholar API (base URL, year range, limit, fields param, auth header). (2) LLMClient internals — constructor params, generate() retry loop (6 attempts, sleep intervals), client.models.generate_content fallback call signature. (3) All configuration parameters from config.py with exact values and environment variable mappings. (4) Logging infrastructure (get_logger, ColoredFormatter, Colors class, CleanNetworkLogs filter). (5) Dependency declarations — requirements.txt (5 declared packages with roles), missing declared dependencies (reportlab — undeclared hard dep for md_to_pdf.py and create_test_pdf.py; pymupdf4llm — undeclared dep for pdf_to_md.py; markdown2 — optional unlisted dep; GAP markers for these). (6) Module-level side effects in config.py (env var suppression, log level overrides). (7) PDF conversion pipeline technical details (Docling chunked processing). (8) Environment variable loading (.env via python-dotenv, GOOGLE_API_KEY, SEMANTIC_SCHOLAR_API_KEY).

writers.scope: Look and feel specification: (1) Page layout — wide layout, page title 'NeurIPS 2026 Checklist Auditor', page icon '🔬'. (2) Sidebar — SIDEBAR_IMAGE (ACM logo URL), SIDEBAR_DESCRIPTION text, width=150. (3) Main title display — TITLE constant text. (4) File uploader widget — accepted extensions (pdf, txt, md), label, spinner/status/success/error widget sequence during processing. (5) Audit results page — success banner, health verdict block (valid/risk states), 4-column metrics row (tiempo_segundos, caracteres_leidos, red_flags_detectadas, plus score from gauge), RAG ficha técnica section, compliance table HTML (_build_table_html with all column headers and risk annotation formats), three expander sections with their labels. (6) Download button — label, file content (Markdown), file name format. (7) SOTA section — header text, button label, spinner text, dataframe/papers display, missing-papers recommendations section. (8) Chatbot section — header, caption, conversation history display, text input key, submit button key, message role display format. (9) Gauge chart — create_gauge_chart(score) Plotly figure with NeurIPS quality tier thresholds and colors. (10) Custom CSS — all CSS selectors and property values exactly as in CUSTOM_CSS constant.

writers.scope: Test scenario specification derived from extraction data. For every business rule documented in the audit pipeline: at least one positive and one negative test scenario with exact input values and expected output. Organized by: (1) Unit tests for LLMClient retry logic (test_llm_retry.py scenarios: mock-based, 6-attempt exhaustion, success-on-Nth-attempt). (2) Integration tests for InformationExtractionSkill section-fragmenting (test_section_splitter.py). (3) Integration tests for RAG logical block splitting (test_rag_logical_splitter.py). (4) Unit tests for AuditState, ExtractedInfo, ChecklistItem models (test_audit_state.py). (5) Integration smoke tests for auditor refactoring (test_auditor_refactor.py: _preprocess_paper returns dict [MARKED AS GAP — method absent in current source]). (6) Import smoke tests (test_imports.py: all frontend modules importable). (7) Skills integration tests (test_skills_integration.py: skills architecture, service initialization). (8) get_checklist_health scenarios (test_checklist_health.py): all 16 items with 'Yes'/'No'/'N/A' answers, pending_justification and missing_evidence edge cases. (9) Scratch scripts as exploratory test cases (test_rag_split.py naive chunks, test_embed.py API response structure).

writers.scope: Domain glossary: (1) Domain Concepts — NeurIPS 2026 reproducibility checklist (all 16 items with labels and definitions), paper_type valid/invalid classification, red_flags dict, audit pipeline phases (FASE 1/1.5/2/2.5/3/4) with domain meaning, MAP/REDUCE extraction strategy, hybrid hyperparameter extraction, RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) as used here. (2) Status Enums — checklist answer values ('Yes'/'No'/'N/A'/'' with meaning and scoring impact), health status ('valid'/'risk'), paper_type enum ('INVALID - Not ML/AI' + valid paper types). (3) Role Constants — not applicable (no user roles in this application). (4) Cross-Domain Equivalences — 'resultado' (audit result dict, also called 'evaluation' in skill context), 'extracted_info' vs 'informacion_extraida' (same object, different keys in different contexts), 'puntuacion' vs 'score' (gauge input vs internal), 'md_text' (session state key) vs 'paper_text' (skill context key). (5) All named constants with provenance: CHECKLIST_KEYS (16 values), CHECKLIST_LABELS, model name constants (6 values + 2 aliases), temperature constants (3 values), API endpoint strings, SEMANTIC_SCHOLAR_* constants.

writers.scope: Hierarchical module catalog. Four top-level modules: (1) backend_core (backend/common/, backend/services/) — 12 files; source cluster cluster_backend_core_01. Services: PaperAuditor (6-phase orchestrator), PaperChatbot/Chatbot alias, SotaAnalyzer, convert_pdf_to_markdown. Common: LLMClient, all prompt template functions, config constants, get_logger. (2) backend_skills (backend/skills/) — 7 files; source cluster cluster_backend_skills_01. Exported skills (15): BaseSkill, CompositeSkill, InformationExtractionSkill, ReproducibilityEvaluationSkill, MetricsCalculationSkill, MetadataAggregationSkill, ConversationalResponseSkill, ContextValidationSkill, ThematicCoverageSkill, QueryGenerationSkill, SemanticScholarSearchSkill, CoverageGapAnalysisSkill, CrossValidationSkill, LimitationsQualityDetectionSkill, SoftwareVersionDetectionSkill, HardwareDetailDetectionSkill. Non-exported skills (HybridHyperparameterExtractionSkill, ChecklistVerificationSkill, 9 regex detection skills). (3) frontend (frontend/) — 14 files; source cluster cluster_frontend_01. Components: audit_results.py, chatbot.py, file_uploader.py, gauge_chart.py, sota_section.py. Utils: scoring.py, session_state.py. Styles: custom_css.py. Config: config.py. Entry: app.py. (4) root (top-level scripts, tests/, scratch/) — 21 files; source cluster cluster_root_tests_scratch_01. Test files: test_auditor_refactor.py, test_imports.py, test_skills_integration.py (tests/), plus scratch/* and backend/scratch/*. Utility scripts: create_test_pdf.py, list_models.py, md_to_pdf.py, pdf_to_md.py, app.py (legacy entry point).

COMPLETENESS REQUIREMENTS (non-negotiable):
a) modules array MUST be ['backend_core', 'backend_skills', 'frontend', 'root'] — derived from cluster_id prefixes.
b) cross_module_summary MUST count every edge where from-node module != to-node module.
c) Every service class (PaperAuditor, PaperChatbot, SotaAnalyzer, LLMClient, convert_pdf_to_markdown) MUST appear as a node of type 'service'.
d) Every skill class MUST appear as a node of type 'service' (or 'skill' if type taxonomy expanded).
e) Every frontend component MUST appear as a node of type 'screen' (for UI rendering functions) or 'service' (for utility functions).
f) Markdown ↔ JSON parity: one #### heading per node in the markdown.

g) Node IDs: 'service:PaperAuditor', 'service:LLMClient', 'service:InformationExtractionSkill', 'screen:audit_results', 'external:GoogleGeminiAPI', 'external:SemanticScholarAPI', 'external:Streamlit', 'config:config_py', 'config:prompts_py' — NEVER opaque numeric IDs.

CRITICAL EDGES to capture: file_uploader→auditor (calls audit()), auditor→InformationExtractionSkill (instantiates/calls), auditor→LLMClient (creates 5 instances), InformationExtractionSkill→GoogleGeminiAPI (via LLMClient.generate), SotaAnalyzer→SemanticScholarAPI (HTTP GET), session_state→auditor (reads), session_state→chatbot (reads), session_state→sota_analyzer (reads), app→file_uploader (calls), app→render_audit_results (calls), app→render_chatbot (calls).
CYCLE to document: st.session_state write cycle — file_uploader writes session_state.resultado, render_audit_results reads session_state.resultado (data flow cycle, informational only).
Produce BOTH markdown and JSON from a single scan to ensure consistent node IDs.
